step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the user actor initiating the request. Route 53 handles the initial DNS resolution. The Application Load Balancer (ALB) routes traffic to EKS. The EKS cluster schedules microservice jobs running on EC2 Spot Instances (AutoScaling). For state resiliency during Spot interruptions, jobs publish messages to SNS which fans out to SQS, allowing new jobs to resume unfinished work.

graph.name: OLX Autos Global Internal Developer Platform
graph.notes: Internal developer platform (Orion) hosting replicas of production environments on 100% Spot instances, scaling from 100 to 10,000+ pods.

nodes.name: Developer
nodes.notes: Requests new environment creation on Orion portal.

nodes.name: Route 53
nodes.notes: Resolves DNS queries for the Orion portal.

nodes.name: ALB
nodes.notes: Distributes incoming traffic to the EKS cluster.

nodes.name: EKS
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Runs 10,000+ pods. Orchestrates microservices and jobs.

nodes.name: Spot Instances
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: 100% Spot instances utilizing AWS Node Termination Handler.

nodes.name: SNS
nodes.notes: Receives events when microservice jobs are created.

nodes.name: SQS
nodes.notes: Stores unfinished job state to ensure consistent execution.

edges.notes: Routes creation requests to EKS workloads.
Launches jobs running on Spot instances.
Triggers event on job launch or interruption.
Fans out job messages to queue.
New jobs watch queue to resume interrupted tasks.
